introSection: Mega Zeph was a wooden roller coaster previously located at Six Flags New Orleans in New Orleans, Louisiana, USA. It was designed by Custom Coasters International, Inc. and opened on May 20, 2000.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Mega Zeph took its name from the original Zephyr coaster that operated in the now-defunct Pontchartrain Beach, which was also located in New Orleans.

The roller coaster opened with Jazzland on May 20, 2000. Upon opening, it was the longest roller coaster at the park.

Mega Zeph and the rest of the park were closed in August 2005 due to water damage from Hurricane Katrina. Demolition began in late 2024.

The ride's sign is now at the National Roller Coaster Museum.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content:
The trains were built by Gerstlauer.

After the closure of Six Flags New Orleans, at least one of the ride's trains was sent to Six Flags St. Louis and is currently stored under Batman The Ride.

fullText:
Mega Zeph was a wooden roller coaster previously located at Six Flags New Orleans in New Orleans, Louisiana, USA. It was designed by Custom Coasters International, Inc. and opened on May 20, 2000.

Mega Zeph took its name from the original Zephyr coaster that operated in the now-defunct Pontchartrain Beach, which was also located in New Orleans.

The roller coaster opened with Jazzland on May 20, 2000. Upon opening, it was the longest roller coaster at the park.

Mega Zeph and the rest of the park were closed in August 2005 due to water damage from Hurricane Katrina. Demolition began in late 2024.

The ride's sign is now at the National Roller Coaster Museum.



The trains were built by Gerstlauer.

After the closure of Six Flags New Orleans, at least one of the ride's trains was sent to Six Flags St. Louis and is currently stored under Batman The Ride.
